evidence_quotes.quote: Currently there is nothing stopping publishers from cancelling a digital game purchases after the fact.
digital ownership of a game should be a persistant consumer right
"end of life" plans for games with online functionality.

paragraphs.text: Currently there is nothing stopping publishers from cancelling a digital game purchases after the fact. In my opinion, digital ownership of a game should be a persistant consumer right, like it would if you buy for example a board game. The main reason for publishers to revoke access to games after purchase is a lack of "end of life" plans for games with online functionality. If ownership of games would be mandated by laws, developers would have to have "end of life" plans for their games. This could include providing server software or making multiplayer peer to peer.
